title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model comprises only 10% of development success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises 90%. The video explores the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, context engineering as a core skill, and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles.
writeup: Cole Medin breaks down Google's playbook on agentic engineering, centering on the claim that the AI model accounts for only 10% of development success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, and verification) accounts for the other 90%. The video maps a spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and frames context engineering as the core skill that distinguishes the two. It also covers the role shift from conductor to orchestrator. The takeaway: durable results come from the harness around the model, not the model alone.
